domain: Bhutanese/Tibetan prayer flag (Buddhist tradition): 5 colors strict order Blue-White-Red-Green-Yellow; each color = element (sky/air/fire/water/earth) + cardinal direction + wisdom; pattern of all 5 in fixed order encodes ritual semantic; rearrangement is improper.
specific_mechanism: Fixed-ordered 5-symbol scheme with multi-axis multi-meaning per symbol; pattern of all 5 in order encodes full ritual semantic; order significance enforced.
llm_application: PRAYER-FLAG 5-axis ordered evaluation-diagnostic: for any LLM evaluate on 5 FIXED ORDERED axes (BLUE: closed-world factuality, WHITE: open-world factuality, RED: reasoning consistency, GREEN: safety/harmlessness, YELLOW: instruction-following). Each axis reports 3-component sub-score (correctness, calibration, robustness). Total diagnostic = 5×3 = 15-d vector with PRESCRIBED ORDER. Downstream axes evaluated ONLY if upstream axes pass minimum thresholds (e.g., reasoning meaningful only after factuality ≥ τ). Differs from (a) HELM (unordered many axes), (b) MMLU (single axis), (c) Cognitive Diagnostic Models (multi-attribute no order), (d) multi-dim GEC (5 metrics one task) by combining (i) FIXED ORDER 5 axes + (ii) 3-COMPONENT per axis + (iii) DEPENDENCY-CONDITIONAL.
novelty_claim: HELM has many axes unordered. MMLU single axis. CDM multi-attribute unordered. PRAYER-FLAG recombines (a) FIXED-ORDERED 5 axes + (b) 3-COMPONENT per axis + (c) DEPENDENCY-CONDITIONAL evaluation. Three known components combined; not wholly new.
content_words: bhutanese tibetan prayer flag color stack
